{"description":"dispatched_by: mayor

Replace EffectFrame-rendered desktop names and global main.qml (+/- buttons) with per-tile OffscreenQuickScene overlays in the desktop grid effect.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- src/effects/desktopgrid/desktopgrid.h — add QVector<OffscreenQuickScene*> m_tileOverlays, QVector<TileOverlayBridge*> m_tileBridges
- src/effects/desktopgrid/desktopgrid.cpp — createTileOverlays(), destroyTileOverlays(), updateTileOverlayGeometry(), modify setup(), finish(), setupGrid(), paintScreen(), windowInputMouseEvent()
- NEW: src/effects/desktopgrid/tile_overlay.qml — Item with desktop name Text, TextInput for editing, MouseArea for hover
- src/effects/desktopgrid/CMakeLists.txt — install tile_overlay.qml

NEW C++ CLASS (inner class in desktopgrid.h):
class TileOverlayBridge : public QObject {
    Q_OBJECT
    Q_PROPERTY(int desktop READ desktop CONSTANT)
    Q_PROPERTY(QString desktopName READ desktopName WRITE setDesktopName NOTIFY desktopNameChanged)
    Q_PROPERTY(int totalDesktops READ totalDesktops NOTIFY totalDesktopsChanged)
};

QML STRUCTURE (tile_overlay.qml):
Item {
    id: root
    property alias bridge: internal.bridge
    MouseArea { id: hoverArea; hoverEnabled: true; anchors.fill: parent }
    Text { id: nameLabel; text: bridge.desktopName; anchors.centerIn: parent; visible: !nameInput.activeFocus }
    TextInput { id: nameInput; text: bridge.desktopName; visible: activeFocus; onAccepted: { bridge.desktopName = text; focus = false } }
}

KEY DETAILS:
- Create overlays in setup() after setupGrid()
- Position via updateTileOverlayGeometry() called from setupGrid() and paintScreen()
- Tile geometry = QRect(scalePos(screenTopLeft, desktop, screen), scaledSize[screen] * scale[screen])
- Opacity = timeline.currentValue()
- Forward mouse events to tile overlays BEFORE handling desktop clicks
- Forward key events to focused TextInput (detect editing state)
- Remove old desktopNames EffectFrame list
- Remove old m_desktopButtons (global add/remove)

SOURCE: ~/ws/kwin-dev/ubuntu-src/kwin-5.24.7/src/effects/desktopgrid/
PLAN: ~/ws/kde_gt/doc/plan.md section 6b (Phase C)"}

{"id":"hq-bh8","title":"C3: Per-tile delete button","description":"Add hover-reveal close button (top-right) to each desktop tile overlay. Clicking removes the desktop with spatial neighbor stitching.

DEPS: C1 (hq-6fo) must land first — this extends tile_overlay.qml.

FILES TO MODIFY:
- src/effects/desktopgrid/desktopgrid.cpp — slotRemoveSpecificDesktop(int desktop) with neighbor stitching
- src/effects/desktopgrid/tile_overlay.qml — add close button

QML CLOSE BUTTON:
Rectangle {
    anchors { top: parent.top; right: parent.right; margins: 4 }
    width: 20; height: 20; radius: 10
    visible: hoverArea.containsMouse && bridge.totalDesktops > 1
    Text { text: 'x'; anchors.centerIn: parent }
    MouseArea { anchors.fill: parent; onClicked: bridge.removeDesktop() }
}

C++ REMOVAL LOGIC:
1. Get desktop spatial neighbors (above/below/left/right)
2. Stitch: if has left+right neighbors -> set left.right=right, right.left=left
3. Stitch: if has above+below neighbors -> set above.below=below, below.above=above
4. Move windows from deleted desktop to preferred neighbor (right > below > left > above > desktop 1)
5. VirtualDesktopManager::removeVirtualDesktop(id)
6. Grid auto-updates via updateSpatialLayout() + setupGrid()
7. Rebuild tile overlays

TileOverlayBridge ADDITION:
Q_INVOKABLE void removeDesktop();

{"id":"hq-9aw","title":"D-fix2: Spatial map writes to __default__ when Activities not yet initialized","description":"activeSpatialMap() falls back to __default__ key when Activities::self() is null or current() is empty. During startup or in edge cases, this causes neighbor data to land in the wrong map, making spatial links appear in all activities. Fix: 1) In activeSpatialMap(), when activities are available, NEVER fall back to __default__. If current() is empty, return a temporary/empty map or wait. 2) In save(), iterate ALL maps in m_spatialMaps and save each to its proper file. Currently save() always writes m_spatialMaps[defaultActivityKey()] to kwinrc and the default JSON, even if the actual data was written to an activity-specific map. 3) Consider removing the __default__ concept entirely — if activities are enabled, always use the activity ID as key. Only use __default__ if KActivities is completely disabled. Files: virtualdesktops.cpp (activeSpatialMap, save, load).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"rastislav.marko@panzarobotics.com","created_at":"2026-03-17T13:56:45Z","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-03-17T13:56:45Z"}
